description_long: This object is a compact, tactical shotgun with a matte black finish and a utilitarian design. It features a single, cylindrical barrel with a perforated heat shield along the top, and a prominent, box-shaped magazine extending downward in front of the trigger. The stock is skeletal and angular, likely made for lightweight maneuverability, and includes a triangular cutout for reduced weight. The receiver is rectangular with visible screws and a simple ejection port, while the fore-end is minimal, lacking a visible pump or lever. The trigger guard is elongated and slightly squared, providing ample space for gloved fingers. Overall, the shotgun has a rugged, industrial appearance suitable for urban environments.
description: A compact, matte black tactical shotgun with a skeletal stock, box magazine, and a perforated barrel heat shield. Its design is rugged and utilitarian, ideal for urban use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, skeletal stock, box magazine, and perforated barrel heat shield.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular barrel opening and the width of the magazine and receiver.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with the skeletal stock and magazine clearly visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the open triangular stock and the alignment of the barrel and receiver.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with a perforated heat shield
attribute_trigger_guard_design: elongated, squared trigger guard with ample space and no finger grooves
